description:
Files: runtime/src/heap.rs

Add fields:
  gc_watermark: *mut u8         // 75% of block_end
  last_gc_live_bytes: usize     // exact post-GC live bytes; 0 until first GC
Remove: low_live_streak: u8

Set watermark in Heap::new(), alloc() grow path, gc() after installing to-space.

Remove count_live_bytes_from() pre-pass from gc(). Replace to-space sizing:
  if last_gc_live_bytes > 0 { last_gc_live_bytes * 2 } else { bytes_used() }
After BFS: last_gc_live_bytes = free - to_start. Delete count_live_bytes_from entirely.

Add compile-time constant and enforce at alloc():
  pub const MAX_HEAP_OBJECT_BYTES: usize = 64;
  assert!(size <= MAX_HEAP_OBJECT_BYTES, ...)
Note: alloc_map(3+ entries) and alloc_bitstring(>40 bytes) already violate this; the assert surfaces violations immediately. Those callers need follow-on redesign.

Tests: watermark = 75% after new/grow/gc; gc() correct without pre-pass; last_gc_live_bytes = free-to_start after gc(); alloc(65) panics.
title: fz-sched.5: Heap foundation — watermark, last_gc_live_bytes, 64-byte limit
